_comment: Docs legacy-ref allowlist gate (t174). The gate scans docs/**/*.md + core/templates/*.md + each harness/<h>/skills/aidlc/SKILL.md (derived FROM DISK) and FAILS unless every surviving retired-token line is pinned here by exact file + line text, with the pinned count <= ceiling. FOUR retired-token lanes: (1) `aidlc-docs` — the flat record layout relocated to aidlc/spaces/<space>/intents/<slug>-<id8>/; (2) bare `--init` — the retired aidlc command; (3) dotted rules dirs `.claude/rules/` | `.kiro/steering/` | `.codex/aidlc-rules/` — the method/rules layer relocated to aidlc/spaces/<space>/memory/ (graph.ts MEMORY_SEGMENTS), so these survive ONLY in native-include prose (the `.claude/rules/aidlc.md` @-import stub, the Kiro `.kiro/steering/` resources glob, the packager rename narrative); (4) `*-learnings.md` filename — learnings collapsed into practices in memory/{team,project}.md (aidlc-learnings.ts), so there are ZERO legitimate survivors. EXCLUDES dist/** (generated; package.ts --check proves parity) and core/aidlc-common/** (the engine-parsed stage tree's aidlc-docs reroot is a separate deferred sweep). Adding an entry requires a visible diff here; blanket-allowlisting trips the ceiling. See docs/reference/07-sensor-system.md, docs/reference/14-claude-features.md, docs/harness-engineering/06-sensors.md.

allowed.text: | **Standing rules** (the layer chain) | `aidlc/spaces/<active-space>/memory/` (via `.claude/rules/aidlc.md` @-import stub) | `aidlc/spaces/<active-space>/memory/` (via Kiro resources glob) | `aidlc/spaces/<active-space>/memory/` (via Kiro resources glob) | `aidlc/spaces/<active-space>/memory/` (via `AIDLC_RULES_DIR`) | `aidlc/spaces/<active-space>/memory/` (via `instructions` glob) |
allowed.why: native-include mapping: names the .claude/rules/aidlc.md @-import stub that pulls the relocated memory tree (correct, not stale)

allowed.text: This implementation reads behavioral rules from the active space memory layer at `aidlc/spaces/<active-space>/memory/`, pulled into Claude's context via the `.claude/rules/aidlc.md` @-import stub. One file per layer of the inheritance chain:
allowed.why: names the .claude/rules/aidlc.md @-import stub (the native include, correct)

allowed.text: **Why the org/team files stay lean:** Claude Code loads the space memory files (via the `.claude/rules/aidlc.md` @-import stub) into each conversation, including non-AI-DLC ones. Keeping the shipped layers to concise, topical structure avoids polluting regular development sessions. Detailed methodology that the upstream specification places in rules instead lives in `.claude/knowledge/aidlc-shared/` or in SKILL.md and stage-protocol.md, loaded only when `/aidlc` is active.
allowed.why: names the .claude/rules/aidlc.md @-import stub (the native include, correct)

allowed.text: | Rules | `aidlc/spaces/<active-space>/memory/*.md` (via `.claude/rules/aidlc.md` @-stub) | Every conversation | Minimal guardrails; self-learning corrections |
allowed.why: names the .claude/rules/aidlc.md @-import stub (the native include, correct)

allowed.text: 9. **Minimal rules** -- Only guardrails (~35 lines total) live in the active space memory layer (`aidlc/spaces/<active-space>/memory/`, pulled in via the `.claude/rules/aidlc.md` @-import stub). Everything else (verification, brownfield safeguards, audit format, adaptive patterns) lives in `knowledge/aidlc-shared/` or is embedded in SKILL.md/stage-protocol.md. This prevents context bloat in non-AI-DLC conversations since rules are always loaded.
allowed.why: names the .claude/rules/aidlc.md @-import stub (the native include, correct)

allowed.text: | Submodules | If a `.gitmodules` is present, reports how many submodule paths are declared and how many are uninitialized, naming `git submodule update --init --recursive` when any are (advisory - never fails) |
allowed.why: the `--init` is part of the canonical `git submodule update --init --recursive` remedy (the doctor Submodules row names it), NOT the retired aidlc `--init` command

allowed.text: - A parseable `.gitmodules` with at least one submodule path entry is a brownfield signal (repo metadata declares code even when the submodule dirs are uninitialized). When submodule paths are uninitialized, the scan warns and names `git submodule update --init --recursive` - surfaced in the `WORKSPACE_SCANNED` event (`Submodules` field + `Details` remedy) and on birth stdout so the conductor can relay it; languages stay as scanned
allowed.why: the `--init` is part of the canonical `git submodule update --init --recursive` remedy the workspace-detection scanner names, NOT the retired aidlc `--init` command
